rerun_reason: Initial audit skipped the three reference files in .claude/skills/website-page-reviewer/references/ and ran a partial banned-vocab grep. This rerun applies the audit-framework / source-audit-protocol / visual-audit-checklist rubrics in full.

phase_2_structural.page_chrome_required: all 9 items present (topnav, crumb, section-label, h1.display, footer copyright, /auth.js, Tailwind CDN, /style.css, body)
phase_2_structural.forbidden_chrome: all absent (no Author/Date/Version line in hero, no Owner-version line in footer)
phase_2_structural.section_label_contiguity: §01 → §06 contiguous (no gaps after §07 Sources drop in v0.9.1)
phase_2_structural.version_pill: v0.9.0 in nav (slightly stale after v0.9.1 commit; nav pill is purely informational so LOW)

exhaustive_check_hero.claim: MVP live for the GJ-cluster pilot · Apr-2026
exhaustive_check_hero.source: Screenshots PDF img-000 shows 5 leads in 'GJ cluster' with April-2026 dates
Sheet1 actual: 6 Done (1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8) · 2 In Progress (2, 9) · 1 Phase 2 (7 AI Feasibility marked Out of scope)

exhaustive_check_hero.claim: 5 platform integrations (Board, Sirion, R-Dash, SAP, Retail Vista)
exhaustive_check_hero.source: Sheet2 row 3 Integrations lists 6 systems: Retail Vista, Board, FR Portal, R-Dash, SAP, Sirion. Page §06A merges FR Portal/SAP into one row but the hero list omits FR Portal entirely
exhaustive_check_hero.verdict: DRIFT — hero name-list omits FR Portal

spot_checks_body.source: PRD §Status Column lists 11 (Draft, New, Under BT validation, Under Negotiations, Under BT approvals, Under Apex approval, Confirmed Properties, RFC offered, RFC under fit-outs, Operational, Rejected)
PRD §Lead Details point 2c — Set 1 (Overview, Site Assessment, Validation) + Set 2 (Approvals, Legal, Construction, Payments) = 7
PRD §Progress Timeline widget · Site Details / Validations / Approvals / Legal / Fit-Out / Operational / Rent Declaration

spot_checks_body.claim: 11 Business Approval cards ending at Apex (§04)
spot_checks_body.source: PRD §Approval tab lists NSO Team, RE-Team, Format Head, Format CFO, NSO Head, Offline CEO, Business CFO, Business CEO, RRVL CFO Office, RRVL CLT, Apex = 11

spot_checks_body.claim: AI Feasibility blocked on Retail Vista (§02 row 7)
spot_checks_body.source: PRD §Business Feasibility-AI Scoring: 'This is dependent on us getting information from another tool called Retail Vista which is still under development.'

phase_3_source_traceability.score_rationale: Two hero-level numerical drifts (7 vs 6 Done; 5 vs 6 integrations name-list incomplete) cap source traceability at 78. Body claims (8 spot-checks) all verified.

phase_4_tone_of_voice.banned_adjective_inflators: clean (transformative, world-class, cutting-edge, game-changing, next-generation, best-in-class, leading, premier, innovative, robust)
phase_4_tone_of_voice.banned_hedge_words: clean (perhaps, could potentially, arguably, it appears that)
phase_4_tone_of_voice.banned_filler_verbs: clean (false-positive on 'drive-time' which is a geo compound noun, not the verb 'drive')
phase_4_tone_of_voice.banned_trailing_connectors: clean (moreover, furthermore, additionally, in addition, importantly)
phase_4_tone_of_voice.banned_constructions_§3: clean (Strong interest in, has shifted the mindset, This is not X. It is Y., In summary)
phase_4_tone_of_voice.date_format: all dates use Mon-YYYY (Apr-2026) — partial DD-MMM-YYYY format. Tone-of-voice §4 prescribes DD-MMM-YYYY but ALP source has no day-precise date for MVP launch (only month-level). Acceptable.
phase_4_tone_of_voice.indian_number_format: no Indian-context monetary numbers on page; not applicable
phase_4_tone_of_voice.western_number_format: approximations not present (no ~ marks); not applicable
phase_4_tone_of_voice.engineering_jargon_in_hero: clean — hero uses NSO, GJ-cluster, MVP, all Reliance/business vocabulary not engineering jargon
phase_4_tone_of_voice.reliance_entity_casing: RRVL, JPL, Apex, Reliance Retail all spelled and cased correctly
phase_4_tone_of_voice.fynd_platform_casing: Retail Vista referenced correctly (capitalised, two words)
phase_4_tone_of_voice.reliance_owned_framing: outcomes attributed to Reliance NSO process; Fynd named only in footer
phase_4_tone_of_voice.emojis_exclamation_marks: none in user-facing prose (false-positives in ! grep are HTML <!doctype, ::after, etc.)

phase_5_honesty_register.pill_presence: all 9 §02 initiatives + 10 §06A items + 7 §06B items carry pills
phase_5_honesty_register.pill_source_match: all match source: Done = plan marks Done; In Progress = plan marks In Progress; Phase 2 = plan marks Out of scope; Planned = plan Sheet2 has no start dates; Roadmap = explicitly disclaimed as direction-not-commitment
phase_5_honesty_register.forward_looking_phases_without_dates: §06A 'Phase 2 plan · scoped, sequenced, not yet started' — no per-item date but the section header carries the meta-date (Phase 2). Acceptable per §06 Vision/Roadmap convention.
phase_5_honesty_register.vision_vs_live_drift: §06B Roadmap intent explicitly disclaims 'no PRD or named owner today'. No drift.

phase_6_visual.network_audit: 11 of 11 requests = 200/304. Zero 4xx/5xx. Verified via list_network_requests.
phase_6_visual.console_audit: 1 message — standard Tailwind CDN dev warning (acceptable noise per checklist).
phase_6_visual.rendering_desktop_1280x900: Hero H1 + status pills + first stat tile visible above the fold. All status pills colour-correct. Stat tiles 4-up grid clean.
phase_6_visual.rendering_mobile_375x812: H1 readable; pills wrap; stats stack to single column; no overflow.
phase_6_visual.lightbox: Verified click → display:flex via evaluate_script. ESC + close button + overlay click all close. JS-fallback graceful (target=_blank href works without JS).

phase_7_cross_page.mega_menu_drift: ALP appears as 'ALP' (no mono-suffix) consistently across 30+ pages. No drift.
phase_7_cross_page.home_page_card: index.html:611-613 carries pill 'LIVE · MVP', title 'ALP · Asset Lifecycle Platform', body matches restructured framing. No drift.
phase_7_cross_page.catalog_row: catalog/index.html:273 uses 'Asset Lifecycle Platform', status text matches page, owner cell '—'. No drift.
phase_7_cross_page.stale_planning_word_sweep: grep 'Asset Lifecycle Planning' across all .html files returns zero hits. Old name fully expunged.
phase_7_cross_page.sibling_page_backlinks: 30+ pages link to /alp via mega-menu only (no body-text references with stale stats). Clean.

findings.issue: Hero stat tile says '7 Done · 2 In Progress'; hero subhead says '7 of 9 Phase 1 initiatives Done'. Counted from §02 table: actual is 6 Done · 2 In Progress · 1 deferred-to-Phase-2 (initiative 7 AI Feasibility, plan-marked 'Out of scope'). Off-by-one drift on a hero stat — exactly the kind of inconsistency Apex catches.
findings.fix: Stat tile context line: change '7 Done · 2 In Progress' → '6 Done · 2 In Progress · 1 deferred'. Subhead: change '7 of 9 Phase 1 initiatives Done' → '6 of 9 Phase 1 initiatives Done · 1 deferred to Phase 2 (AI Feasibility)'.

findings.issue: Hero says '5 platform integrations (Board, Sirion, R-Dash, SAP, Retail Vista)'. Project plan Sheet 2 row 3 lists 6 integrations: Retail Vista, Board, FR Portal, R-Dash, SAP, Sirion. Page §06A correctly references all 6 (FR Portal merged into the SAP/Payments row), but the hero name-list omits FR Portal entirely.
findings.fix: Either expand to 'integrations with Board, Sirion, R-Dash, SAP, FR Portal, Retail Vista' (6 named, drops the count) OR keep the count and write '5 integration tracks' to honour the §06A row count.

findings.issue: Nav version pill reads v0.9.0; page is now at v0.9.1 (post-Sources-block drop). Pill is informational only and the register accepts lazy version-pill updates, so LOW.

findings_remaining_from_v2.issue: §03 = Lead lifecycle override of canonical §03 = Architecture
findings_remaining_from_v2.rationale: Documented in spec §9 D7. Per audit-framework §1A, documented overrides are not findings.
Per visual-audit-checklist Phase B, this is acceptable noise — the only register-wide pattern.

delta_from_v2.what_changed: Found 3 new findings (H4, M4, L3) that the rushed v2 audit missed. v2 reported 95/A; this rerun reports 90/A−. Source Traceability dimension dropped from 95 → 78 because of the two hero-level numerical drifts.
delta_from_v2.why_v2_missed: v2 spot-checked source traceability informally rather than building a full claim-list and verifying each hero stat against source. The audit-framework / source-audit-protocol references that were supposed to gate Phase 3 were never read.

strengths: Page chrome 100% compliant: every required item present, every forbidden item absent.
Tone of voice 96/100: exhaustive grep across §7 + §3 banned lists returns clean. Engineering jargon stays out of hero.
All 11 network requests 200/304. Zero broken images. Lightbox click-to-overlay verified live in Chrome DevTools.
8 spot-checked body claims (11 statuses, 7 tabs, 7 timeline stages, 11 approval cards, Lead ID format, AI Feasibility blocked on Vista) all verified against PRD or Screenshots PDF.
Cross-page consistency now clean: home card, IP catalog row, mega-menu mono-suffix all aligned. 'Asset Lifecycle Planning' string fully expunged from repo.
ship_readiness: Page passes overall (90/A−). One HIGH finding (H4 hero count drift) is auto-fixable; recommend fixing before sharing with Apex. M4 + L3 also mechanical.
